flatshading_coherence.label: RENDU 3D — flat-shading cohérence
flatshading_coherence.finding: 20 MeshStandardMaterial constructors confirmed with flatShading:true (lines 37, 38, 56, 62, 68, 97, 104, 128, 148, 157, 182, 202, 233, 234, 308, 310, 486, 569, 590, 607). 5 MeshBasicMaterial instances correctly exempt (visible:false hit targets at lines 214, 273, 684 + lightMat at line 235 + cauldron steamMat is PointsMaterial — correct). Zero flatShading omissions on visible MeshStandardMaterial. Minor deduction: MeshBasicMaterial lightMat (door sliver, line 235) renders as flat emissive — no flatShading needed, architecturally correct. Score 9.0 rather than 10.0 because shelfMat (line 569 in createPotionBottles) has flatShading:true but metalness field is absent — benign since default metalness=0 is assumed, but pattern is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase.
flatshading_coherence.problem: MerlinLairScene.ts:569 — shelfMat in createPotionBottles() omits metalness:0.0 field (all other MeshStandardMaterial constructors include it explicitly). Cosmetically inert but breaks pattern consistency.

zfighting_residual.label: RENDU 3D — z-fighting résiduel après C43b
zfighting_residual.finding: BUG-L-07-GHOST was the target fix for C44. Code audit at MerlinLairScene.ts:516 and :539 confirms the fix from C43b was APPLIED: glow is now added to group (line 516: group.add(glow)) and steamPoints to group (line 539: group.add(steamPoints)). CauldronSystem interface at line 476 declares glow:THREE.PointLight. The group.visible=false at LairGLBAssets.ts:38 now cleanly kills body, legs, glow, and steam in one toggle. Z-fighting residuals from prior cycles: sol_pierre offset at y=-4.98 unchanged (PASS), mur_pierre tiles at z=-9.76 offset from procedural wall at z=-10 (PASS). Score 8.0 not 10.0: one structural concern remains — cauldronHit (MerlinLairScene.ts:682-687) is a SphereGeometry r=1.2 added directly to scene (not to cauldron.group), meaning it survives cauldron.group.visible=false. If GLB loads and procedural group is hidden, the invisible hit target for cauldron zone remains floating at world (2,-3.0,-7) — correct behaviour (zone stays interactive), but the position does not track the GLB cauldron's actual rim. GLB positioned at y=-4.65 with scale 0.72 places the opening at approx y=-4.0; the hit target center at y=-3.0 is ~1u above the GLB rim — hover zone is above the actual object.
zfighting_residual.problem: MerlinLairScene.ts:686 — cauldronHit center at y=-3.0 is ~1u above the GLB cauldron rim (GLB at y=-4.65, scale 0.72, rim at approx y=-4.0). Hit target spatial mismatch causes invisible hover zone to float above the visible cauldron.

materiaux_qualite.label: RENDU 3D — matériaux qualité (roughness/metalness palette Celtic)
materiaux_qualite.finding: Stone walls: roughness=0.95, metalness=0.0 — correct, aged limestone. Floor: roughness=0.9, metalness=0.0 — correct flagstone. Beams: roughness=0.85, metalness=0.0 — correct weathered oak. Iron cauldron: roughness=0.5, metalness=0.6 — correct cast iron. Door iron straps: roughness=0.5, metalness=0.7 — correct wrought iron. Crystal: roughness=0.05, metalness=0.1 with transparency=0.82 — correct translucent crystal. Scroll border: roughness=0.7, metalness=0.1 — correct gilded leather. Potion bottles: roughness=0.1 transparent glass — correct. Bone/skull: roughness=0.7, metalness=0.0 — correct dry bone. Palette covers: iron (0x1a1a1a/0x2a2828), wood (0x3d2b1a/0x4a3520), stone (0x3a3228), crystal (0x9060cc) — cohesive Celtic low-poly register. Minor deduction: crystal roughness=0.05 is glass-like rather than quartz-like (0.2-0.3 would read more naturally as milky ogham stone). Pedestal metalness=0.3 reads as polished stone — contextually fine but slightly anachronistic for Celtic stonework.
materiaux_qualite.problem: MerlinLairScene.ts:151 — crystalMat roughness=0.05 renders the Ogham stone as a glass orb rather than quartz crystal. Celtic Ogham stones are rough-hewn; roughness=0.25 would better evoke carved quartzite.

lighting_dramaturgie.label: RENDU 3D — lighting dramaturgie (9 sources total)
lighting_dramaturgie.finding: Light inventory: (1) AmbientLight 0x1a1008 intensity=0.5 — dark warm base, correct for candlelit cave. (2) Key PointLight 0xff6618 intensity=1.1 at (0,8,-2) — overhead warm fire, correct. (3) Rim PointLight 0x6699cc intensity=0.55 at (8,2,7) — cool blue from door side. (4) Fill PointLight 0xcc8833 intensity=0.4 at (-9,0,-5) — warm left wall. (5) Crystal PointLight 0x9060cc intensity=2.5 at (5,-1,-4) — purple oracle. (6) CauldronSystem glow 0x22cc44 intensity=1.1-1.3 at (2,-3.2,-7) — green bubble. (7) Door PointLight 0xffdd66 intensity=3.0 at (-9.8,-1.8,5.5) — forest light under door. (8) Candle shared PointLight 0xff9933 intensity=1.8 at single shared position — covers 3 candles. (9) CandleSystem has sharedLight at one position (line 316) covering all three candles (line 314 comment confirms: 'saves 2 GPU light slots'). Total: 9 active lights (1 ambient + 8 point). Key/fill/rim ratio analysis: key(1.1) vs fill(0.4) = 2.75:1 — correct for dramatic but not flat. Rim cool vs key warm — emotionally correct (cold forest night vs warm cave). Cauldron green (0x22cc44) is folkloric Celtic — correct. Crystal purple (0x9060cc) — correct Ogham mysticism. Door amber (0xffdd66) pulling toward forest — effective CTA reinforcement. Deductions: (a) candle sharedLight at a SINGLE position covers 3 geographically dispersed candles — candles at (-5,-1.6,-7), (0,-4.6,-8.5), (3,-3.0,-6) are lit by one point, meaning two candles cast light from the wrong source position. This is the stated tradeoff (saves 2 slots) but creates spatially incoherent shadows. (b) No back-wall rim — the 16u back wall gets only the key light spill, creating a uniform dark band that reduces depth perception.
lighting_dramaturgie.problem: MerlinLairScene.ts:316 — single shared candle PointLight covers 3 candles at disparate positions (-5/-1.6/-7, 0/-4.6/-8.5, 3/-3.0/-6). Shadows and light falloff are spatially incoherent on two of the three candles. Acceptable trade-off for mobile but is an auditable compromise.

glb_coverage.label: ASSETS — couverture GLB (6 assets + procedural fallbacks)
glb_coverage.finding: 6 GLB files confirmed in dist/: cauldron_merlin.glb, bougie.glb, table_druidique.glb, bibliotheque.glb, sol_pierre.glb, mur_pierre.glb. All 6 have load handlers in LairGLBAssets.ts (lines 27, 42, 52, 60, 69, 79). Procedural hide on success: cauldronGroup.visible=false (line 38 — PASS post C43b), mapGroup.visible=false (line 56 — PASS), shelfGroup.visible=false (line 64 — PASS), floorMesh.visible=false (line 73 — PASS), wallsGroup.visible=false (line 103 — PASS). Bougie: no procedural group to hide — GLB instances are added directly alongside procedural candle bodies. This means bougie.glb + procedural candle body overlap if both load. The candle bodies are created in createCandles() and added to scene; no bougie hide mechanism exists. Minor deduction for this overlap. All .catch() fallbacks are silent — correct for progressive enhancement.
glb_coverage.problem: LairGLBAssets.ts:42-49 — bougie.glb loads 3 clone instances but there is no proceduralGroups.candleGroup to hide. Procedural candle bodies (created in createCandles) remain visible alongside GLB bougie clones — double-mesh candles when both are present.

proportions_geometriques.label: ASSETS — proportions géométriques (mur_pierre tile math)
proportions_geometriques.finding: mur_pierre.glb is authored as a tile with 5 stone rows x 4 stone columns (126 polys). Model-space normalized height ~1u. At scale.set(4,16,1): tile is 4u wide x 16u tall. Stone cell width = 4u/4cols = 1u per stone. Stone cell height = 16u/5rows = 3.2u per stone. Aspect ratio = 1:3.2 (vertical elongation). This is RESIDUAL-L-SCALE-01 from C43 — the known ceiling of the clone-and-scale approach. The C43 report confirmed this is a PASS for demoability (improvement from 1.87 horizontal stretch to 3.2 vertical) but a FAIL for AAA stone masonry (natural stone aspect ratio 1:1 to 1:1.5). The tile coverage math is geometrically correct: back wall 6×4u=24u covering x=-12..+12 (PASS), side walls 5×4u=20u covering z=-10..+10 (PASS). Score 7.5: structurally sound, coverage correct, but vertical stone elongation remains visually suboptimal at AAA standard.
proportions_geometriques.problem: LairGLBAssets.ts:83,89,97 — scale.y=16 on all mur_pierre tiles yields 1:3.2 stone aspect ratio (5 rows × 16u/5 = 3.2u tall vs 1u wide). Requires Blender re-authoring of mur_pierre.glb to fix natively (tile should be 4u wide × 16u tall in model space to achieve 1:1 cells at scale(4,16,1)).

bundle_gzip.label: WEB PERF — bundle gzip sizes
bundle_gzip.finding: Three JS bundles measured via gzip compression: three-core-5sOazYjm.js = 134,687 bytes gzip (131.5 KB). index-BNbhrdtv.js = 44,028 bytes gzip (43.0 KB). minigames-8Cwb1cNx.js = 20,735 bytes gzip (20.3 KB). Total gzip transfer = 199,450 bytes (194.8 KB). Target was <200 KB — PASS by 5.2 KB margin (2.6% headroom). Breakdown: Three.js core dominates at 67% of total bundle. Main scene code at 22%. 14 minigames combined at 10% — excellent code-splitting result. Risk: margin is tight. Each new Three.js feature import (EffectComposer, PMREMGenerator, etc.) could push three-core over the limit. Current chunk strategy (three-core separate from index) is correct.
bundle_gzip.problem: Bundle total gzip = 194.8 KB, only 5.2 KB under the 200 KB target. Adding any significant Three.js import (e.g., EffectComposer ~15 KB gzip) would breach the target. No specific file:line — architectural risk flag.

fps_budget.label: WEB PERF — FPS budget (draw calls + mobile risk)
fps_budget.finding: Draw call estimate: walls procedural = 3 boxes + mortar lines (7 planes) + floor + ceiling + 4 beams + 2 cross-beams = 18. When GLB tiles load: 16 mur_pierre clones replace the 3 wall boxes (net +13). GLB floor/table/shelf/cauldron add ~4 separate scene objects. Candles: 3 clones (+3). Procedural objects: crystal (sphere+cylinder+PointLight), door (~7 meshes), bookshelf (~25 books+frame), skull (2), potion bottles (6), dust motes (Points), cauldron steam (Points), density objects (LairDensity). Rough total: ~80-100 draw calls when all GLBs loaded. Nine lights (8 PointLights + 1 AmbientLight). Three.js default max lights is 4 in MeshStandardMaterial without custom shader — however, Three.js r150+ handles multiple PointLights via per-object light list (5-light-per-object default). With 8 PointLights, any single mesh sees at most 5 simultaneously — spatially dispersed setup means most surfaces only receive 2-3 active lights in practice. Risk: on lower-end mobile (Mali-G52, Adreno 505), 8 point lights with shadow = severe; without shadows (confirmed: no shadow casting configured — no castShadow/receiveShadow calls in codebase) the budget is manageable. Fog (Fog near=12, far=28) clips distant geometry early — positive contribution to mobile FPS.
fps_budget.problem: No specific line — architectural: 8 PointLights active simultaneously in an enclosed scene. Without shadow maps (confirmed none configured) this is mobile-safe, but adding any castShadow=true to even one light (natural next step for dramatic improvement) would cause a severe mobile performance cliff.

feedback_zones.label: UX/ACCESSIBILITY — feedback zones (5 zones, hover, toast, door CTA)
feedback_zones.finding: 5 interactive zones confirmed: map (mapHit scroll), crystal (sphere hit target), bookshelf (invisible hitTarget 3.5×5.5×0.15 at z=-7.65), door (invisible hitTarget 0.5×7×3.5 at z=5.5), cauldron (SphereGeometry r=1.2 at y=-3.0). All wired to interactives[] array (MerlinLairScene.ts:697-703). Hover emissive: all objects with a visualMesh or direct mesh apply emissiveIntensity=0.65 on hover (line 741) and restore to baseEmissive on exit (line 735). Cursor changes to 'pointer' on hover (line 742). Toast system: role=status + aria-live=polite + aria-atomic=true on each toast (main.ts:198-200) — WCAG AA compliant live region. All 5 ZONE_LABELS present and unique. Door label 'Entrer dans la forêt / → Commencer l'aventure' intact. Toast duration 1800ms with 220ms fade-out — correct (not too fast, not annoying). Door click triggers toast immediately then resolve() — correct CTA confirmation. Minor deduction: cauldron zone (line 702) has baseEmissive:0.0 but the cauldron mesh (iron, dark) has no emissive channel set — applyHoverTo() applies emissiveIntensity but if the material has no emissive color (default 0x000000), the visual feedback is invisible. Player hovering cauldron gets no visible emissive response.
feedback_zones.problem: MerlinLairScene.ts:702 — cauldronHit entry has baseEmissive:0.0, but the cauldron iron material (ironMat, line 486) has no emissive color set (default black). applyHoverTo() sets emissiveIntensity=0.65 on hover but black emissive at any intensity = no visible change. Cauldron hover has zero visual feedback.

aria_mobile.label: UX/ACCESSIBILITY — ARIA + mobile (role=status, touchmove)
aria_mobile.finding: Toast: role=status + aria-live=polite + aria-atomic=true (main.ts:198-200) — correct WCAG 2.1 pattern for non-interruptive live regions. Minigames canvas ARIA: all 14 minigames (mg_apaisement through mg_volonte) confirmed with canvas.setAttribute('role','application') and canvas.setAttribute('aria-label', descriptive text). MinigameBase result overlay (MinigameBase.ts:108): aria-live=assertive — correct for outcome announcement. Timer progressbars in mg_combat_rituel, mg_herboristerie, mg_sang_froid, mg_traces: role=progressbar + aria-valuemin/max/now — correct. 14/14 minigames covered. touchmove passive:false: no explicit touchmove listener found in main.ts grep results. The canvas is the Three.js renderer's domElement; Three.js by default registers pointer events (pointermove/pointerdown) not touchmove directly in r150+. Risk of passive:false violation is LOW in current implementation. Minor deduction: mg_negociation.ts (line 111-112) has aria-label but the label text 'choisissez les bons mots pour persuader' could be more specific about the input mechanic. Minor prose issue.
aria_mobile.problem: MinigameBase.ts:108 — outcome overlay uses aria-live=assertive, which is correct for final results, but intermediate state changes within the minigame (score updates, timer ticks) are not announced. Screen reader users receive no live feedback during gameplay — only the final result. This is a gap vs WCAG 2.1 AA for games.

game_feel_flow.label: GAME FEEL — flow entrée → zone → run (timing)
game_feel_flow.finding: Entry sequence: revealFromBlack(800ms) at main.ts:161 — 800ms fade-in, correct for scene establishment. Zone hover: emissive transition is immediate (no tween) — slightly abrupt for AAA standard but acceptable for low-poly aesthetic. Toast animation: double-RAF opacity transition (main.ts:212) + 1800ms display + 220ms fade = ~2240ms total toast life. This is within the comfortable reading window (WCAG: 5s minimum for 3-word phrases; actual phrases are ~5 words each — PASS). Door click flow: showZoneToast('door') + resolve() fires in same tick. Then: cancelAnimationFrame(rafId) → cutToBlack() → 300ms await → lair.dispose() → wrapper.style.display='none'. Total transition: toast flash (~200ms visible) + 300ms black = ~500ms perceived. This is appropriately snappy (FromSoftware standard: ~300-500ms transition to black). The 300ms cutToBlack window is hard-coded (line 233) — it matches the SceneTransition animation expectation. Smooth flow overall. Minor deduction: toast for door zone appears immediately before cutToBlack, meaning the text 'Entrer dans la forêt' is visible for only ~200ms before black — too brief to be read comfortably. The toast confirmation serves a design purpose but the timing is too tight.
game_feel_flow.problem: main.ts:222-234 — door toast appears then resolve() fires immediately, leading to cutToBlack ~200ms after toast becomes visible. Player cannot read 'Entrer dans la forêt / → Commencer l'aventure' before black. Suggest await Promise for 400ms after door toast before resolve().

lore_celtic.label: GAME DESIGN — cohérence lore Celtic (4 sublabels + door)
lore_celtic.finding: 5 ZONE_LABELS audited: (1) map = 'Carte des Biomes / Les chemins se révèlent en marchant' — biome map discovery mechanic, neutral future tense, Celtic wandering motif. Authentic. (2) crystal = 'Pierre des Oghams / Les runes ne répondent pas encore' — Ogham stones as divination medium, dormancy motif, correct. In Irish mythology, Oghams are carved on standing stones and 'speak' at initiation. (3) bookshelf = 'Journal de Merlin / Les pages sont encore en gestation' — 'en gestation' is botanically evocative (seeds germinating), nature metaphor appropriate to druidic tradition. (4) cauldron = 'Chaudron Druidique / L'anam doit d'abord s'éveiller' — 'anam' is the Irish/Gaelic word for 'soul' or 'life-force', used correctly here as the animating principle that must be awakened before the cauldron functions. This is authentic — the Cauldron of the Dagda in mythology required the worthy to awaken it. Highest lore authenticity in the set. (5) door = 'Entrer dans la forêt / → Commencer l'aventure' — Brocéliande forest (Arthurian/Breton Celtic), direct and functional. The arrow '→' is the only non-Celtic element but serves UX clarity. Minor deduction: 'Les chemins se révèlent en marchant' (map sublabel) is philosophically correct but lacks Celtic specificity — could reference 'les slighe' (ancient Irish roadways) or 'les voies druidiques' for deeper authenticity.
lore_celtic.problem: main.ts:186 — map sublabel 'Les chemins se révèlent en marchant' is thematically sound but generically philosophical. Celtic specificity would be elevated by referencing the druidic road network (slighe) or the concept of 'dán' (destiny-path). Minor lore depth gap.

verified_fixed.description: Cauldron glow PointLight and steamPoints particles now added to group (MerlinLairScene.ts:516 group.add(glow), :539 group.add(steamPoints)). When GLB cauldron loads and cauldronGroup.visible=false fires, all procedural cauldron elements — body, legs, glow, steam — are cleanly hidden. No ghost glow or floating steam survives the hide.
verified_fixed.evidence: MerlinLairScene.ts:516 confirms group.add(glow) — not scene.add. MerlinLairScene.ts:539 confirms group.add(steamPoints) — not scene.add. LairGLBAssets.ts:38 cauldronGroup.visible=false now kills all 5 cauldron sub-elements.

residual_or_new_bugs.title: cauldronHit center 1u above GLB cauldron rim — hit zone spatially misaligned
residual_or_new_bugs.detail: cauldronHit (MerlinLairScene.ts:682-687) is a SphereGeometry r=1.2 centered at world y=-3.0. GLB cauldron_merlin.glb is positioned at y=-4.65 with scale 0.72 — the visible cauldron rim sits at approximately y=-4.0 (top of a sphere-body scaled 0.72 from a ~1u model). Hit zone center at y=-3.0 places the hover sphere 1u above the actual cauldron rim. Player must hover above the cauldron — the hit region extends from y=-4.2 to y=-1.8. The cauldron body peak (y≈-4.0) is barely inside the lower edge of the hit sphere. Hover works but is disconnected from the visible affordance.
residual_or_new_bugs.affected_lines: MerlinLairScene.ts:686 (cauldronHit position y=-3.0) vs LairGLBAssets.ts:35 (GLB y=-4.65)
residual_or_new_bugs.recommendation: Move cauldronHit center to y=-4.0 to align with GLB cauldron rim. Also reduce radius from 1.2 to 0.9 for tighter affordance: cauldronHit.position.set(2, -4.0, -7) and SphereGeometry(0.9, 8, 6). No other changes needed.

residual_or_new_bugs.title: Cauldron hover emissive has zero visual effect — ironMat has no emissive color
residual_or_new_bugs.detail: The cauldron entry in interactives[] (MerlinLairScene.ts:702) specifies baseEmissive:0.0 and no visualMesh. applyHoverTo() attempts to set emissiveIntensity on the cauldron hit target mesh (MeshBasicMaterial visible:false). MeshBasicMaterial does not support emissiveIntensity. Additionally, the iron cauldron body/legs use ironMat (line 486: color=0x1a1a1a, roughness=0.5, metalness=0.6) which has NO emissive field — default emissive is 0x000000. Setting emissiveIntensity=0.65 on black emissive produces no visible change. Player receives zero visual feedback on cauldron hover — no cursor change to pointer either since cauldronHit is the hit target but is not the mesh targeted by applyHoverTo.
residual_or_new_bugs.affected_lines: MerlinLairScene.ts:702 (cauldron interactives entry — no visualMesh), :486 (ironMat — no emissive), :720-723 (applyHoverTo targets obj.visualMesh ?? obj.mesh — for cauldron, obj.mesh is the invisible hit sphere with MeshBasicMaterial)
residual_or_new_bugs.recommendation: Add visualMesh reference to cauldron interactives entry: { mesh: cauldronHit, zone: 'cauldron', hovered: false, visualMesh: cauldron.group.children[0] as THREE.Mesh, baseEmissive: 0.0 }. Also add emissive channel to ironMat: emissive: 0x00aa33, emissiveIntensity: 0.0. On hover set to 0.3 — subtle green pulse matching cauldron's glow colour.

residual_or_new_bugs.title: bougie.glb overlaps procedural candle bodies — no procedural hide on GLB load
residual_or_new_bugs.detail: LairGLBAssets.ts:42-49 loads bougie.glb and adds 3 clones to scene. createCandles() in MerlinLairScene.ts creates procedural candle bodies (CylinderGeometry flame cones + PointLight). No hide mechanism exists for the procedural candles when bougie.glb loads. If GLB loads successfully, both meshes occupy the same world positions: CANDLE_POSITIONS in LairGLBAssets.ts = [(-5,-4.85,-7), (0,-4.85,-8.5), (3,-4.85,-6)]. The procedural candle bodies are created at different y heights (candlePositions in MerlinLairScene.ts:296 = [-5,-1.6,-7], [0,-4.6,-8.5], [3,-3.0,-6]). Heights differ significantly (e.g., candle 0: GLB y=-4.85 vs procedural y=-1.6 = 3.25u gap). GLB bougies and procedural candle meshes are at different heights — no direct z-fighting, but both are visible simultaneously creating doubled/mismatched candle geometry.
residual_or_new_bugs.recommendation: Add candlesGroup parameter to LairProceduralGroups interface and pass the candle meshes group from createCandles(). Hide on bougie GLB success. Alternatively: remove procedural candle bodies entirely (keep only the candle PointLights for warm lighting) and rely on bougie.glb for geometry.

residual_or_new_bugs.title: Stone vertical elongation 1:3.2 — residual after tiled-grid fix
residual_or_new_bugs.detail: mur_pierre.glb tile: 5 rows × 4 cols stones. At scale.set(4,16,1): stone cell = 1u wide × 3.2u tall. Aspect ratio 1:3.2. Improvement from C42 (1.87 horizontal) to C43 (3.2 vertical). Still sub-AAA for masonry. Threshold for demoability: PASS. Score floor capped at 7.5 for this criterion.
residual_or_new_bugs.recommendation: Re-author mur_pierre.glb in Blender to output a tile 4u wide × 16u tall in model space (rearrange stones into taller rows). At that native size, scale.set(4,16,1) maps 1:1 geometrically.

residual_or_new_bugs.title: bougie.glb clones share material references — independent flicker impossible
residual_or_new_bugs.detail: LairGLBAssets.ts:44 gltf.scene.clone(true) shares materials. No material.clone() traversal. Benign until candle flicker animation is added.
residual_or_new_bugs.recommendation: Pre-condition on T-BOUGIE-FLICKER task: traverse cloned scene and call child.material = child.material.clone() for each Mesh.

residual_or_new_bugs.title: Door toast visible for only ~200ms before cutToBlack — unreadable
residual_or_new_bugs.detail: main.ts:222-233: door click calls showZoneToast('door') then resolve() immediately. The Promise resolves in the same execution tick, cancelAnimationFrame fires, then cutToBlack() starts. The toast becomes visible (double-RAF ~32ms) but cutToBlack overlaps before 400ms reading time. 'Entrer dans la forêt / → Commencer l\'aventure' is a 5-word confirmation but the player has <200ms effective reading time.
residual_or_new_bugs.recommendation: Introduce a 400ms delay between door toast and resolve: setTimeout(resolve, 400). This keeps the total transition under 750ms (toast 400ms + cutToBlack 300ms) — below the perceptible 'slow' threshold while giving reading time.

player_journey_post_fix.entry: Player boot → main menu → Lair Hub. revealFromBlack(800ms) opens on the stone chamber. Back wall reads as tiled masonry (3.2× vertical stones, improved from C42 horizontal stretch). Warm amber key light overhead, green cauldron glow in the mid-distance, purple crystal glimmer to the right.
player_journey_post_fix.exploration: Player moves mouse. Crystal sphere (right, foreground) brightens on hover — emissive pulse visible. Toast: 'Pierre des Oghams / Les runes ne répondent pas encore'. Atmospheric. Lore-accurate dormancy. Bookshelf (right wall, mid) highlights shelf frame on hover. Toast: 'Journal de Merlin / Les pages sont encore en gestation'. Distinct and earthy.
player_journey_post_fix.cauldron_interaction: Player approaches cauldron (back-left). Hit zone at y=-3.0 is 1u above GLB cauldron rim — player must hover slightly above the visible cauldron. No visible emissive feedback (BUG-L-CAULDRON-HOVER). Toast still fires: 'Chaudron Druidique / L\'anam doit d\'abord s\'éveiller'. Steam and glow now cleanly hidden with group if GLB present — no ghost glow (C43b fix confirmed working).
player_journey_post_fix.map_table: Map scroll hover: emissive warms from 0.15 to 0.65. Toast: 'Carte des Biomes / Les chemins se révèlent en marchant'. Smooth.
player_journey_post_fix.door: Player clicks door (left wall). Toast fires: 'Entrer dans la forêt'. CutToBlack begins ~200ms later — toast barely readable (BUG-DOOR-TOAST-TIMING). 300ms black. lair.dispose(). Game starts. Transition is functionally complete.
player_journey_post_fix.net_impression: C44 is a clean cycle. BUG-L-07-GHOST fully resolved — no ghost effects. Lore quality: highest yet (anam reference is genuinely Celtic-grade). Two new MEDIUM bugs found (cauldron hit misalignment, cauldron hover visibility) that were masked by the ghost bug in C43. Bundle perf at target boundary — requires monitoring. Score 8.6/10 — PROCEED threshold cleared.

score_justification.flatshading_coherence: 9.0 — 20/20 MeshStandardMaterial with flatShading:true. One minor inconsistency (metalness field omitted in createPotionBottles shelfMat line 569) prevents perfect score.
score_justification.zfighting_residual: 8.0 — BUG-L-07-GHOST confirmed fixed. New finding: cauldronHit center 1u above GLB rim. No active z-fighting. Score reflects one spatial alignment issue.
score_justification.materiaux_qualite: 8.5 — Palette is cohesive Celtic low-poly (iron/wood/stone/crystal). Crystal roughness=0.05 reads as glass orb rather than carved quartz. All other materials contextually correct.
score_justification.lighting_dramaturgie: 7.5 — 9 lights with appropriate emotional colour (warm fire, cool forest rim, green Ogham, purple oracle, amber door). Key:fill ratio 2.75:1 correct. Single shared candle light is spatially incoherent for 2 of 3 candles — known mobile budget trade-off.
score_justification.glb_coverage: 9.5 — All 6 GLB loaded and procedural hide-on-success working. Bougie has no candle body hide (minor overlap issue).
score_justification.proportions_geometriques: 7.5 — Stone coverage math correct. 1:3.2 vertical elongation is residual known ceiling from C43.
score_justification.bundle_gzip: 9.5 — 194.8 KB total gzip, 2.6% margin under 200 KB target. Three.js chunk strategy correct.
score_justification.fps_budget: 8.0 — ~80-100 draw calls, 8 PointLights without shadows — mobile-safe current state. One shadow light added would cause mobile cliff.
score_justification.feedback_zones: 9.0 — 5/5 zones with toast feedback. Toast ARIA correct. Cauldron hover has zero visual emissive response (BUG-L-CAULDRON-HOVER).
score_justification.aria_mobile: 8.5 — 14/14 minigames with role=application + aria-label. Toast live region correct. Screen reader has no in-game feedback during minigame play — gap vs WCAG AA for interactive applications.
score_justification.game_feel_flow: 8.5 — Entry and zone navigation feel smooth. Door toast is too brief before cutToBlack (~200ms effective reading time vs recommended 400ms).
score_justification.lore_celtic: 9.0 — 4/5 sublabels are high-fidelity Celtic (anam=soul, Oghams dormancy, pages in gestation). Map sublabel is philosophically correct but lacks Celtic specificity.

next_steps.task: Fix BUG-L-CAULDRON-HOVER: Add emissive channel to ironMat (emissive:0x00aa33, emissiveIntensity:0.0) and wire cauldron interactives entry with visualMesh pointing to the cauldron body mesh. Ensures green emissive pulse on hover matching the cauldron's existing glow colour.
Fix BUG-L-CAULDRON-HIT: Move cauldronHit center to y=-4.0 (from y=-3.0) and reduce radius to 0.9 (from 1.2) to align hover zone with GLB cauldron rim.
Fix BUG-DOOR-TOAST-TIMING: Delay resolve() by 400ms after door click to allow toast reading before cutToBlack. Total transition stays under 750ms.
next_steps.effort: XS — wrap resolve in setTimeout(resolve, 400) in main.ts

next_steps.task: Fix BUG-L-BOUGIE-OVERLAP: Add candlesGroup to LairProceduralGroups and hide procedural candle bodies when bougie.glb loads. Keep candle PointLights active (they are in scene directly, not in candlesGroup).
next_steps.effort: S — interface + group wiring in MerlinLairScene.ts + LairGLBAssets.ts

next_steps.task: Address RESIDUAL-L-SCALE-01: Re-author mur_pierre.glb in Blender to output a tile with native 4u×16u proportions (rearrange stone rows to 4 tall rows of 4 stones each = 1:1 aspect at scale(4,16,1)).

Monitor bundle gzip: after next Three.js import addition, re-measure. Set a CI size budget check (vite-bundle-visualizer or rollup-plugin-visualizer) to alert when three-core chunk exceeds 130 KB gzip.

evidence: LairGLBAssets.ts — 6/6 GLB assets with load handlers and procedural fallbacks
LairGLBAssets.ts:81-103 — mur_pierre tile grid coverage math verified correct
main.ts:185-191 — 5/5 ZONE_LABELS unique, Celtic register, door CTA intact
main.ts:198-200 — toast ARIA: role=status + aria-live=polite + aria-atomic=true
14/14 minigames — role=application + aria-label on canvas element
Bundle gzip: index=43.0KB + minigames=20.3KB + three-core=131.5KB = 194.8KB (target <200KB — PASS)
9 light sources: AmbientLight(1) + PointLight(8) — no shadow maps — mobile-safe
